title: Syzygium surigaense
taxonomy: Syzygium surigaense is classified under the family Myrtaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. This family is part of the order Myrtales and is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Myrtaceae is characterized by its woody plants with aromatic leaves and showy flowers. The family includes many species of trees, shrubs, and herbs, some of which are cultivated for their fragrant leaves or showy flowers. intro: The Syzygium surigaense, also known as the Mindanao water apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. This plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in protecting against various diseases and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Syzygium surigaense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
